title: EU signs trade agreements with Mexico
content: Following the signature of the Modernised Global Agreement and the Interim Trade Agreement, the EU and Mexico follow their respective procedures to ratify the agreements.
snippet: Following the signature of the Modernised Global Agreement and the Interim Trade Agreement, the EU and Mexico follow their respective procedures to ratify the agreements.

title: EU and Mexico deepen long-term ties at a historic summit
content: Today, the EU and Mexico have taken a major step forward in their partnership by signing the Modernised Global Agreement (MGA) and interim Trade Agreement (iTA).
snippet: Today, the EU and Mexico have taken a major step forward in their partnership by signing the Modernised Global Agreement (MGA) and interim Trade Agreement (iTA).

title: Commission launches EUR 16.5 million call to dismantle criminal networks
content: The European Commission has published a call for proposals to co-fund projects aiming to fight organised crime and drug trafficking, as well as trafficking in human beings. With EUR 16.55 million, it will support law enforcement but also public bodies working to dismantle criminal networks.

title: Ten years of the GDPR: your data, your rights
content: The GDPR turns ten. Learn how this landmark data protection law changed life online forever, what rights it gives you, and how the EU protects you in the digital world.
snippet: The GDPR turns ten. Learn how this landmark data protection law changed life online forever, what rights it gives you, and how the EU protects you in the digital world.

title: Commission publishes 2025 report on the Digital Markets Act implementation
content: The Commission published its third annual report outlining the progress towards achieving the objectives of the DMA to support fair and contestable digital markets in the EU.
snippet: The Commission published its third annual report outlining the progress towards achieving the objectives of the DMA to support fair and contestable digital markets in the EU.

title: EU economy forecast to slow down amid rising inflation following energy shock
content: This year’s Spring Economic Forecast reflects the impact on the EU economy of the recent outbreak of conflict in the Middle East. In the wake of the conflict’s energy shock that drove up energy commodity prices, the forecast projects weaker economic activity as inflation rises.

title: Integration at Local Level: EUR 77 Million under AMIF Specific Action
content: The Commission has launched a call for expression of interest under a specific Action of the Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund (AMIF) which will allow Member States to support Local and Regional Authorities (LRAs) in implementing integration measures for third-country nationals.

title: Wide participation at RIMA meeting strengthens EU Research and innovation coordination
content: 130 policy makers gather to discuss access to excellence.
snippet: 130 policy makers gather to discuss access to excellence.

title: Integration at Local Level: EUR 77 Million under AMIF Specific Action
content: The Commission has launched a call for expression of interest under a specific Action of the Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund, to support integration measures implemented by Local and Regional Authorities in EU Member States, with an indicative overall budget of EUR 77 million.

title: A jobseeker’s guide to working across Europe
content: Millions of Europeans are crossing borders for better careers, short-term gigs and new adventures with EURES by their side. You can do it, too!
snippet: Millions of Europeans are crossing borders for better careers, short-term gigs and new adventures with EURES by their side. You can do it, too!

title: Science comes to Town: Taking European research and innovation where it matters most
content: The second edition of "Science Comes to town" was formally launched to bring science closer to citizens across four European cities in 2027.
snippet: The second edition of "Science Comes to town" was formally launched to bring science closer to citizens across four European cities in 2027.

title: From local action to global impact: EU delivers on biodiversity ahead of CBD COP17
content: Protecting biodiversity is increasingly recognised not only as an environmental necessity, but also as an economic and security imperative.
snippet: Protecting biodiversity is increasingly recognised not only as an environmental necessity, but also as an economic and security imperative.

title: CBD COP17: Addressing the biodiversity financing gap
content: Around 140 stakeholders gathered in Brussels to find innovative ideas as to how to scale up private investment in biodiversity.
snippet: Around 140 stakeholders gathered in Brussels to find innovative ideas as to how to scale up private investment in biodiversity.

title: VAT in the Digital Age: 2026 Work Programme available
content: The European Commission has published the work programme for VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA), covering implementation activities planned for 2026.
snippet: The European Commission has published the work programme for VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA), covering implementation activities planned for 2026.

title: Commission publishes guidance to strengthen Europe's resilience by clarifying the application of EU water laws
content: The guidance document ensures a more harmonised implementation of EU water laws by reducing compliance uncertainties.
snippet: The guidance document ensures a more harmonised implementation of EU water laws by reducing compliance uncertainties.

title: Commission approves €13 million French State aid for fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to Middle East crisis
content: Approved €13 million French aid scheme to support fishing companies to address the increase in fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis. The scheme was approved under the Middle East Crisis Temporary State Aid Framework (METSAF) adopted by the Commission on 29 April 2026.
snippet: Approved €13 million French aid scheme to support fishing companies to address the increase in fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis. The scheme was approved under the Middle East Crisis Temporary

title: Commission awards €400 million to European projects to decarbonise heat production
content: Today, the European Commission has selected 65 projects under the Innovation Fund Heat Auction, the first ever EU-wide auction to accelerate the deployment of innovative clean heat technologies across European industry.

title: Directorate-General of Research and Innovation at the EU Green Week 2026
content: DG Research and Innovation organises two side events focused on scaling up nature-based solutions and strengthening water resilience.
snippet: DG Research and Innovation organises two side events focused on scaling up nature-based solutions and strengthening water resilience.

title: Copenhagen Forum underlines importance of energy infrastructure
content: Policymakers, industry representatives and stakeholders met in Copenhagen on 21-22 May for the 12th Energy Infrastructure Forum with a near unanimous message that the ongoing conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine highlight that energy infrastructure is more important than ever.

title: EU expands support for workers at risk of job loss
content: The Council has adopted new rules to expand the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund for Displaced Workers (EGF). For the first time, workers facing imminent job loss due to company restructuring will be eligible for support.

title: Commission sends Statements of Objections to several companies concerning synthetic turf cartels
content: The European Commission has informed several companies active in the synthetic turf sector of its preliminary view that they have breached EU antitrust rules by colluding to distort competition in synthetic turf for sports pitches in the Netherlands and Germany.

title: EU’s new Waste Shipment Regulation and DIWASS digital platform go live
content: The Digital Waste Shipment System (DIWASS) will be key for the EU's circular economy transition and a decisive step towards full digitalisation.
snippet: The Digital Waste Shipment System (DIWASS) will be key for the EU's circular economy transition and a decisive step towards full digitalisation.

title: Solidarity Lanes: Latest figures – April 2026
content: Latest figures on Ukrainian exports and imports via the EU-Ukraine Solidarity Lanes: new transport routes established in the face of Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine.
snippet: Latest figures on Ukrainian exports and imports via the EU-Ukraine Solidarity Lanes: new transport routes established in the face of Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine.

title: Commissioner Kos in Montenegro to mark 20th anniversary of restoring independence
content: Today, Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos is in Montenegro to mark the 20th anniversary of the restoration of the country’s independence and to reaffirm the EU’s strong support for Montenegro’s EU accession process.

title: Expert group recommendations on making smart charging and demand response easier
content: Two expert groups of the European Commission, alongside the European Coalition of the Willing on Bi-Directional Charging, presented a comprehensive set of recommendations to simplify data exchange for flexibility, smart charging, and bi-directional charging.

title: Celebrating Natura 2000: the world’s largest network of protected natural areas
content: On Natura 2000 Day, we celebrate the EU’s flagship nature network, the largest network of environmentally protected areas in the world
snippet: On Natura 2000 Day, we celebrate the EU’s flagship nature network, the largest network of environmentally protected areas in the world
